learning.summary: Successful operating system installations begin with careful planning. Reviewing storage, licensing, compatibility, backups, and configuration before installation reduces the risk of data loss and installation failures.
learning.explanation:
Before installing or upgrading an operating system, confirm that the correct destination drive has been selected and determine how storage partitions will be configured. Verify that important data has been backed up and that any required license keys or activation information are available.

Installation planning should also include checking hardware requirements, reviewing application and driver compatibility, verifying existing partitions, and ensuring user data and preferences have been preserved when appropriate. Performing these checks before installation helps avoid mistakes that are difficult to reverse.

facts.text: Confirm the correct target drive before installing an operating system.
Plan partition configuration before making changes to storage.
Verify backups, hardware requirements, application compatibility, and license information before installation.

examples.text: Before reinstalling Windows, a technician verifies backups, confirms the installation drive, checks application compatibility, and records the software license key.

notes: User preference preservation should be treated as part of backup and installation planning rather than a standalone concept.

examTips.text: Treat installation planning as a checklist before making destructive changes.

commonMistakes.text: Failing to verify backups before repartitioning.

scenarios.situation: Determine which preparation steps should be completed before performing a clean installation.
scenarios.expectedAction: Identify the correct concept or response.

pbqIdeas.task: Review a pre-installation checklist and identify missing preparation tasks.

reviewNotes: Backup scope, verification, and user preference preservation were expanded beyond the transcript based on discovery guidance and should be reviewed.
Promoted from authored draft as needs-review canonical content.
